finding.title: Letta Client-Server Architectural Lock-in
finding.description: Letta >= 0.6.x has been refactored into a mandatory client-server architecture. The embedded/local mode (create_client()) was removed. Running Letta now requires: (1) a running Letta server (Docker or cloud), (2) asyncpg + PostgreSQL for persistence, or (3) Letta Cloud API key. This makes Letta impossible to deploy in air-gapped or resource-constrained environments.
finding.import_error: cannot import name create_client from letta
finding.server_import_error: No module named asyncpg (required by letta.orm)
finding.severity: CRITICAL - bars use in many enterprise/research environments

comparison.mem0: Fully embeddable, pip install mem0ai, runs in-process
comparison.langmem: Embeddable but embedding API format constraint
comparison.letta: REQUIRES dedicated server process - highest deployment friction

note: This architectural finding is itself a key MemSysBench contribution: Letta has the highest deployment barrier of all tested systems.
